title: TDAS
description: Timing Devices Agnostic Structure (TDAS). A document carries inputs, exactly one of {oscillator, timer, latch} (the field name is the discriminator, the SAS/AAS pattern), and outputs. TDAS is the home for time-base components: frequency-control products (quartz crystals, XO/TCXO/VCXO/OCXO, MEMS and silicon-RC oscillators, programmable oscillators), timers (555-class monostable/astable), and set/reset latches. Every family also carries a simulator-agnostic ideal `behavioral` block, so an anonymous PWM ramp or PCM gate latch inside a CIAS control brick and an orderable SiTime part are the same type. Boundary: PWM/LLC/PFC controller ICs live in CTAS; analog signal-path ICs (op amps, comparators, sample-and-holds) live in AAS; TDAS holds the parts whose defining function is generating or sequencing TIME. Every valid TDAS document is also a valid PEAS document (the `timeBase` branch).

inputs.description: Design requirements and operating points for this time-base part.
inputs.title: inputs

oscillator.description: Crystal / resonator / oscillator: bare quartz crystals and ceramic resonators, packaged XO, TCXO, VCXO, OCXO, MEMS, silicon-RC and programmable oscillators — plus the ideal periodic-source behavioral atom (PWM ramp, clock, resonant drive, voltage-controlled frequency).
oscillator.title: oscillator

timer.description: 555-class timer: monostable one-shot / astable — plus the ideal timer behavioral atom (constant-on-time generation).
timer.title: timer

latch.description: Set/reset latch: discrete SR-latch logic parts — plus the ideal latch behavioral atom (peak-current-mode commutation memory).
latch.title: latch

outputs.description: Computed results per operating point. outputs[i] aligns positionally with inputs.operatingPoints[i].
outputs.title: outputs
